disclaimer: Educational assessment. CARSI is an IICRC-approved CEC provider; this is CARSI-original Australian training, not IICRC certification.
note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: CARSI Carpet Cleaning Practitioner — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: Which carpet fibre demands the most conservative chemistry — acid-side pH and low heat?
You are cleaning an 80% wool / 20% nylon blend. How do you choose your chemistry?
options: Treat it as nylon because nylon is the majority by resilience
Treat it as wool — the more delicate fibre governs (weakest-member rule)
Use the strongest alkaline the nylon allows
Alternate wool and nylon products across the room

questions.questionText: In the CHAT model, the four variables you can trade against one another are Chemistry, Heat, Agitation and…
Which method uses the least moisture and suits interim maintenance of commercial carpet?
What is the correct first step of the cleaning process, before any chemistry is applied?
options: Thorough dry vacuuming to remove dry soil

questions.questionText: Before applying any product to a carpet you should always…
options: Trust the manufacturer label and proceed
Pre-inspect and test in an inconspicuous area
Apply to the most visible stain first

questions.questionText: To treat a spot correctly you should…
options: Rub vigorously from the outside in
Blot — never rub — to preserve pile geometry
Apply heat immediately to every spot

questions.questionText: Mould is present after water exposure. What is the correct drying approach?
options: Run air movers immediately to dry faster
Use air-filtration devices and dehumidifiers only — no air movers until clearance
Open all windows and use fans
Apply more heat to kill the mould
